name: P_element_hotspot_chr2R_42AB
description: Drosophila TE insertion hotspot: P-element insertion hotspot near 42AB cytological band — P-elements transposed into D. melanogaster from D. willistoni in ~1950s, current laboratory strains have 30-50 P insertions. Drosophila genomes carry ~15-20% TE content. Insertion hotspots reflect both target-site preference and lower selection in low-recombination regions. Reads hitting these regions are typically misplaced unless full-length insertion-specific anchors are available.
expected_copy_count: 10-100 copies of the named TE family genome-wide; locally enriched 3-10x baseline
